seoTitle: Redwood Park vs Reeves Plains — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Redwood Park and Reeves Plains. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Redwood Park vs Reeves Plains: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Redwood Park and Reeves Plains.
heading: Redwood Park vs Reeves Plains — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Redwood Park, South Australia and Reeves Plains, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Reeves Plains has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Redwood Park (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Redwood Park or Reeves Plains safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Redwood Park has a flood risk score of 36/100, while Reeves Plains scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Redwood Park scores 40/100 for bushfire risk. Reeves Plains scores 43/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Redwood Park or Reeves Plains?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Reeves Plains has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
